editor_settings.title: Is the Canon G7X Mark III Discontinued? Here’s What Nobody Tells You
editor_settings.content:
1. The Empty Shelves

It happens every single day, someone walks into Best Buy or checks B&H Photo, sees the Canon G7X Mark III listed as "Backordered," and immediately goes to Reddit to ask if Canon has permanently discontinued the camera.

2. The Official Answer

As of late 2026, Canon has NOT officially discontinued the G7X Mark III. It remains an active product in their lineup. The reason you can't find it is entirely due to unprecedented, viral demand.

@rominagafur #g7x

3. The TikTok Effect

The camera became a cultural phenomenon on TikTok for capturing the "2010s flash party aesthetic." Canon, forecasting demand for a 2019 point-and-shoot camera to be extremely low, scaled back manufacturing years ago. When the camera went viral, their factories simply could not catch up.

4. Should You Pay eBay Scalpers?

Absolutely not. Because people think it's discontinued, they are paying $1,200 for a used copy of a $749 camera. Put your name on a waitlist at Adorama or a local camera store. They receive small, consistent batches every few months.

editor_settings.meta_description: You can't find the Canon G7X Mark III in stock anywhere, leading many to believe it's dead. It's not discontinued, it's just a victim of unprecedented viral hype.
